title: erlang:throw/1 — throw exception type
purpose:
Erlang has three exception classes: `error` (from `erlang:error/1`), `throw` (from `erlang:throw/1`), and `exit` (from `erlang:exit/1`). beamr implements `error` and `exit` but not `throw`. The `throw` class is used by Gleam's `rescue` and by OTP's `gen_server` for non-local returns. Without it, any code calling `erlang:throw/1` gets an unresolved import error, and `try/catch` blocks that match on `throw:Reason` never fire.

The key challenge: the current BIF error path always wraps `Err(reason)` as `Exception { class: atom(error), ... }`. For `throw`, the exception class must be `atom(throw)`.
task: Register `erlang:throw/1` as a BIF and add a mechanism for BIFs to specify the exception class. The simplest approach: add an `exception_class` field to `ProcessContext` that BIFs can set before returning `Err`. The interpreter reads this field when constructing the `Exception`, then resets it to `error`.

requirements.title: ProcessContext exception class field
requirements.spec: Add an `exception_class` field to `ProcessContext` that defaults to `ExceptionClass::Error`. BIFs can set it to `ExceptionClass::Throw` before returning `Err`.
acceptance: `ProcessContext` gains `exception_class: ExceptionClass` field (default: `Error`)
`ExceptionClass` enum with variants: `Error`, `Throw`, `Exit`
`ProcessContext::set_exception_class(class)` setter
`ProcessContext::take_exception_class() -> ExceptionClass` reads and resets to `Error`
Existing BIFs (error/1, exit/2) do not set this field and continue to work unchanged

requirements.title: Interpreter reads exception class from ProcessContext
requirements.spec: Modify the BIF error handling path in the interpreter to read `ProcessContext::take_exception_class()` instead of hardcoding `atom(error)` as the exception class.
acceptance: When a BIF returns `Err(reason)`, the interpreter constructs `Exception { class: atom(take_exception_class()), reason, stacktrace }`
If no BIF set the exception class, `take_exception_class()` returns `Error` (backward compatible)
The exception class atom is correctly mapped: `Error` → `atom(error)`, `Throw` → `atom(throw)`, `Exit` → `atom(exit)`
Test: a BIF returning `Err` without setting exception class produces `Exception { class: atom(error) }` (existing behavior preserved)

requirements.title: erlang:throw/1 BIF registration
requirements.spec: Register `erlang:throw/1` as a BIF. The implementation sets `exception_class` to `Throw` on ProcessContext, then returns `Err(reason)`.
acceptance: `throw/1` registered in the BIF registry under the `erlang` module
Implementation: `context.set_exception_class(ExceptionClass::Throw); Err(*reason)`
Import resolution resolves `erlang:throw/1` without unresolved-import errors
Test: calling `throw(hello)` produces `Exception { class: atom(throw), reason: atom(hello) }`

modify: crates/beamr/src/native/bifs.rs (bifs.rs at 625 lines — add ~10 lines for throw/1 registration and handler)

requirements.title: try/catch correctly dispatches throw class
requirements.spec: Verify that the existing `try_case` handler correctly matches `throw` class exceptions. In BEAM semantics, `try_case` writes `(class, reason, stacktrace)` to X registers. The `class` must be `atom(throw)` for thrown exceptions, not `atom(error)`.
acceptance: A `try/catch` block that catches `throw:Reason` matches when `erlang:throw(Reason)` is called inside the try
A `try/catch` block that only catches `error:Reason` does NOT match a `throw` exception
Test: `try throw(42) catch throw:V -> V end` returns `42`
Test: `try throw(42) catch error:V -> V end` does not catch — exception propagates

modify: crates/beamr/src/interpreter/opcodes/messaging.rs (only if changes needed — may just need tests)

requirements.title: erlang:exit/1 uses ExceptionClass::Exit
requirements.spec: While we're adding the exception class mechanism, wire `erlang:exit/1` (self-exit, different from `exit/2` which sends to another process) through the same path for consistency.
acceptance: `erlang:exit/1` sets `ExceptionClass::Exit` on ProcessContext before returning `Err`
`try exit(reason) catch exit:R -> R end` returns `reason`
Existing exit/2 behavior (sending exit signals to other processes) is unchanged

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the `NativeFn` return type — BIFs still return `Result<Term, Term>`
SHALL NOT implement `erlang:error/2` (error with args) — that's a separate brief
SHALL NOT modify the exception propagation path (try/catch/raise) — only the exception construction at the BIF→interpreter boundary
SHALL verify that existing `try/catch` dispatch correctly matches `throw` class exceptions
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
